    10→- [License](#license)
    11→
    12→This is experiment 1 to explore creating a Vibe Coding (vc) environment.
    13→We will investigate ways of using the dual jj-git repo concept, explored
    14→in [hw-jjg-bot](https://github.com/winksaville/hw-jjg-bot.git) to
    15→initially make it easy to see how the code base evolved. This is
    16→made possible by the fact that we have two repos one with the code
    17→and one with the conversation with the bot.
    18→
    19→I've chosen the jj-git environment because jj provides the concept that
    20→each commit has an immutable changeID as well as the mutable commitID
    21→of git. The idea is that each commit made on repo A writes the
    22→changeID in the commit message to repo B. Thus there is a cross reference
    23→between the two repos and this will allow vc-x1 to show how the repo
    24→evolved and the entity (bot or human) can more clearly understand **how** and
    25→most importantly **why** the code evolved.
    26→
    27→The solution space is wide open, from trivial CLI, web or app based
    28→(mobile/non-mobile). In addition, I could see this as an extension to
    29→existing programming editors like vscode and zed or even creating our
    30→own IDE for vc.
    31→
    32→See [Initial commit with dual jj-git repos](./notes/chores-01.md#initial-commit-with-dual-jj-git-repos)
    33→for how the initial commit was created with the dual jj-git repos. After
    34→doing so and I then created this README.md file.
    35→
    36→## Usage
    37→
    38→```
    39→vc-x1 list [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # List commits in a jj repo
    40→vc-x1 desc [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # Show full description of a commit
    41→vc-x1 chid [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # Print changeID(s) for a revision
    42→vc-x1 show [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # Show commit details and diff summary
    43→vc-x1 finalize --bookmark <B> [OPTS]   # Squash working copy into target
    44→vc-x1 --version                        # Print version
    45→vc-x1 --help                           # Print help
    46→```
    47→
    48→**The `..` notation:** dots on the revision show which direction to
    49→list commits:
    50→
    51→- `x..` — x at top, ancestors below (older commits)
    52→- `..x` — descendants above (newer commits), x at bottom
    53→- `..x..` — both directions, x in the middle
    54→
    55→COMMITS is the total number of commits to show (x is always included).
    56→Without dots, `x` with a count defaults to `x..` (ancestors).
    57→For `..x..`, the budget is split: ancestors get the extra on odd counts.
    58→
    59→```
    60→vc-x1 list -r @              # just @ (1 commit)
    61→vc-x1 list -r @.. -n 5       # @ + 4 ancestors (5 commits, @ at top)
    62→vc-x1 list -r ..@ -n 3       # 2 descendants + @ (3 commits, @ at bottom)
    63→vc-x1 list -r ..@.. -n 3     # 1 descendant, @, 1 ancestor (3 commits)
    64→vc-x1 list -r ..@.. -n 4     # 1 descendant, @, 2 ancestors (4 commits)
    65→```
    66→
    67→### Positional shorthand
    68→
    69→REVISION and COMMITS can be given as positional arguments, omitting
    70→`-r` and `-n`:
    71→
    72→```
    73→vc-x1 list x                # just x (1 commit)
    74→vc-x1 list x 5              # x.. 5 → x + 4 ancestors (5 commits)
    75→vc-x1 list x.. 5            # same as above
    76→vc-x1 list ..x 3            # 2 descendants + x (3 commits)
    77→vc-x1 list ..x.. 3          # 1 descendant, x, 1 ancestor (3 commits)
    78→vc-x1 list ..x.. 1          # just x (1 commit)
    79→```
    80→
    81→**Defaults:** with no arguments, REVISION is `@` and COMMITS is 1
    82→(just the working copy).
    83→
    84→Named flags `-r`/`--revision`, `-n`/`--commits`, and `-R`/`--repo`
    85→take precedence over positional arguments.
    86→
    87→### Multi-repo queries
    88→
    89→The `-R`/`--repo` flag can be repeated or comma-separated to query
    90→multiple repos at once. When multiple repos are given, output is
    91→labeled with bold `=== path ===` headers by default:
    92→
    93→```
    94→vc-x1 chid -R . -R .claude      # repeated flag
    95→vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude         # comma-separated
    96→vc-x1 list @.. 3 -R .,.claude   # works with all read-only subcommands
    97→```
    98→
    99→Control the label between repos with `-l`/`--label` and `-L`/`--no-label`:
   100→
   101→```
   102→vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude            # default label: === path ===
   103→vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude -l "---"   # custom label:  --- path ---
   104→vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude -L         # no label (raw output)
   105→```
   106→
   107→Examples:
   108→
   109→```
   110→$ vc-x1 chid -R . -R .claude
   111→=== . ===
   112→kwoyvposvsmv
   113→
   114→=== .claude ===
   115→zzpksklxyrnw
   116→
   117→$ vc-x1 chid -R . -R .claude -L
   118→kwoyvposvsmv
   119→zzpksklxyrnw
   120→
   121→$ vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude
   122→=== . ===
   123→kwoyvposvsmv
   124→
   125→=== .claude ===
   126→zzpksklxyrnw
   127→
   128→$ vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude -L
   129→kwoyvposvsmv
   130→zzpksklxyrnw
   131→
   132→$ vc-x1 list @.. 3 -R .,.claude
   133→=== . ===
   134→kwoyvposvsmv eae175c3c1b5 (no description set)
   135→pqxtxxpnsmot b0c46930a640 Reorganize notes (0.19.1)
   136→ssokyszzwsxw eeb15bc6d839 Unify .. notation and CLI (0.19.0)
   137→
   138→=== .claude ===
   139→zzpksklxyrnw 2388185e42ee (no description set)
   140→tzupykyyvnrp 81ce22e34d41 Reorganize notes (0.19.1)
   141→slmkmroqtqtp 1c33ccaa567d Unify .. notation and CLI (0.19.0)
   142→
   143→$ vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude -L
   144→kwoyvposvsmv
   145→zzpksklxyrnw
   146→
   147→$ vc-x1 desc -r @- -R .,.claude
   148→=== . ===
   149→pqxtxxpnsmot b0c46930a640 Reorganize notes: move older done items to done.md, update todos (0.19.1)
   150→
   151→    Move completed items [1]-[13] and their references from todo.md to done.md.
   152→    Replace completed jj-organization todo with new items.
   153→
   154→    ochid: tzupykyyvnrp
   155→
   156→=== .claude ===
   157→tzupykyyvnrp 81ce22e34d41 Reorganize notes: move older done items to done.md, update todos (0.19.1)
   158→
   159→    Session: reviewed and committed notes reorganization for 0.19.1.
   160→
   161→    ochid: pqxtxxpnsmot
   162→
   163→$ vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude -l "---"
   164→--- . ---
   165→kwoyvposvsmv
   166→
   167→--- .claude ---
   168→zzpksklxyrnw
   169→```
   170→
   171→Most decoration strings work unquoted (`---`, `===`, `>>>`, `:::`,
   172→`+++`). Use single quotes for strings containing shell metacharacters
   173→like `*`, `!`, `#`, `$`, or `~` (e.g. `-l '***'`). Double quotes
   174→won't protect against `!` (bash history expansion).
   175→
   176→With a single repo (or no `-R`), no label is printed — backward
   177→compatible with previous behavior. Multi-repo is supported for
   178→`chid`, `desc`, `list`, and `show`. `finalize` remains single-repo.
   179→
   180→### finalize
   181→
   182→Solves the session repo coherence problem: when the bot commits `.claude`,
   183→the act of committing generates more session data. `finalize` waits,
   184→then squashes the trailing writes into the target commit.
   185→
   186→By default finalize runs in the foreground. Use `--detach` to run in
   187→the background (the bot uses this so the session can end immediately).
   188→
   189→The bot's last action in a session:
   190→
   191→```
   192→vc-x1 finalize --repo .claude --bookmark main --delay 10 --detach --push
   193→```
   194→
   195→If there is non-written session data after a session ends (e.g.
   196→finalize failed or was skipped), run it manually:
   197→
   198→```
   199→vc-x1 finalize --repo .claude --bookmark main --push
   200→```
   201→
   202→This runs in the foreground, squashes, advances the bookmark, and pushes.
   203→
   204→See [finalize subcommand](./notes/chores-01.md#finalize-subcommand-for-session-repo-coherence)
   205→for full option reference and design details.
   206→
   207→### Testing finalize
   208→
   209→Always test against a throwaway jj repo, never the live workspace.
   210→Use `mktemp -d` for unique directories so results can't be confused
   211→with a previous run:
   212→
   213→```bash
   214→# Create and init a temp repo
   215→dir=$(mktemp -d /tmp/vc-x1-test-XXXXXX)
   216→(cd "$dir" && jj git init)
   217→
   218→# Foreground (default, blocks, logs to test dir)
   219→vc-x1 finalize --repo "$dir" --bookmark main --log "$dir/finalize.log"
   220→cat "$dir/finalize.log"
   221→
   222→# Detached (returns immediately, child logs to test dir)
   223→dir2=$(mktemp -d /tmp/vc-x1-test-XXXXXX)
   224→(cd "$dir2" && jj git init)
   225→vc-x1 finalize --detach --repo "$dir2" --bookmark main --log "$dir2/finalize.log"
   226→cat "$dir2/finalize.log"
   227→```
   228→
   229→The log file shows timestamped (nanoseconds) entries with PIDs, covering
   230→the full flow: `main` entry/exit, `finalize` entry/exit, `detach`
   231→spawn, and `finalize_exec` in the child process.
   232→
   233→## Cross-repo Linking with Git Trailers
   234→
   235→Commits in each repo use [git trailers](https://git-scm.com/docs/git-interpret-trailers)
   236→to cross-reference their counterpart in the other repo. The `ochid`
   237→(Other Change ID) trailer contains a workspace-root-relative path
   238→and jj changeID:
   239→
   240→```
   241→ochid: /.claude/xvzvruqo   # points to a .claude repo change
   242→ochid: /wtpmottv            # points to an app repo change
   243→```
   244→
   245→Paths always start with `/` (the workspace root, i.e. vc-x1).
   246→Each repo has a `.vc-config.toml` that identifies its location
   247→within the workspace, so tools can resolve these paths locally.
   248→
   249→For full details see:
   250→- [Git trailer convention](./notes/chores-01.md#git-trailer-convention)
   251→  — [ochid (Other Change ID)](./notes/chores-01.md#ochid-other-change-id)
   252→  — [ChangeID path syntax](./notes/chores-01.md#changeid-path-syntax)
   253→  — [.vc-config.toml](./notes/chores-01.md#vc-configtoml)
   254→
   255→## jj Tips for Git Users
   256→
   257→If you're coming from git, jj's log output can be surprising compared to
   258→tools like `gitk --all`.
   259→
   260→### Why `jj log` shows fewer commits than `gitk`
   261→
   262→jj tracks *changes* (identified by change IDs), not individual git commits.
   263→When you rewrite a change (`jj describe`, `jj rebase`, `jj squash`, etc.),
   264→jj creates a new git commit and keeps the old one under `refs/jj/keep/*` as
   265→undo history. `gitk --all` sees all of these obsolete commits; `jj log` only
   266→shows the current version of each change.
   267→
   268→### Useful commands
   269→
   270→| Command | Description |
   271→|---------|-------------|
   272→| `jj log` | Show recent visible commits (default revset) |
   273→| `jj log -r ::@` | Show **all** ancestors of the working copy |
   274→| `jj log -r 'all()'` | Show all non-hidden commits (needed if you have multiple heads/branches) |
   275→| `jj obslog -r <change-id>` | Show the evolution history of a single change |
   276→| `jj op log` | Show operation history (each rewrite operation) |
   277→
   278→In a single-branch workflow, `jj log -r ::@` and `jj log -r 'all()'` give
   279→the same result. Use `all()` when you have multiple branches or heads.

file.content:
# vc-x1

- [Overview](#vc-x1)
- [Usage](#usage)
  - [Revision shortcuts](#revision-shortcuts)
  - [finalize](#finalize)
  - [Testing finalize](#testing-finalize)
- [Cross-repo Linking with Git Trailers](#cross-repo-linking-with-git-trailers)
- [jj Tips for Git Users](#jj-tips-for-git-users)
- [License](#license)

This is experiment 1 to explore creating a Vibe Coding (vc) environment.
We will investigate ways of using the dual jj-git repo concept, explored
in [hw-jjg-bot](https://github.com/winksaville/hw-jjg-bot.git) to
initially make it easy to see how the code base evolved. This is
made possible by the fact that we have two repos one with the code
and one with the conversation with the bot.

I've chosen the jj-git environment because jj provides the concept that
each commit has an immutable changeID as well as the mutable commitID
of git. The idea is that each commit made on repo A writes the
changeID in the commit message to repo B. Thus there is a cross reference
between the two repos and this will allow vc-x1 to show how the repo
evolved and the entity (bot or human) can more clearly understand **how** and
most importantly **why** the code evolved.

The solution space is wide open, from trivial CLI, web or app based
(mobile/non-mobile). In addition, I could see this as an extension to
existing programming editors like vscode and zed or even creating our
own IDE for vc.

See [Initial commit with dual jj-git repos](./notes/chores-01.md#initial-commit-with-dual-jj-git-repos)
for how the initial commit was created with the dual jj-git repos. After
doing so and I then created this README.md file.

## Usage

```
vc-x1 list [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # List commits in a jj repo
vc-x1 desc [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # Show full description of a commit
vc-x1 chid [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # Print changeID(s) for a revision
vc-x1 show [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # Show commit details and diff summary
vc-x1 finalize --bookmark <B> [OPTS]   # Squash working copy into target
vc-x1 --version                        # Print version
vc-x1 --help                           # Print help
```

**The `..` notation:** dots on the revision show which direction to
list commits:

- `x..` — x at top, ancestors below (older commits)
- `..x` — descendants above (newer commits), x at bottom
- `..x..` — both directions, x in the middle

COMMITS is the total number of commits to show (x is always included).
Without dots, `x` with a count defaults to `x..` (ancestors).
For `..x..`, the budget is split: ancestors get the extra on odd counts.

```
vc-x1 list -r @              # just @ (1 commit)
vc-x1 list -r @.. -n 5       # @ + 4 ancestors (5 commits, @ at top)
vc-x1 list -r ..@ -n 3       # 2 descendants + @ (3 commits, @ at bottom)
vc-x1 list -r ..@.. -n 3     # 1 descendant, @, 1 ancestor (3 commits)
vc-x1 list -r ..@.. -n 4     # 1 descendant, @, 2 ancestors (4 commits)
```

### Positional shorthand

REVISION and COMMITS can be given as positional arguments, omitting
`-r` and `-n`:

```
vc-x1 list x                # just x (1 commit)
vc-x1 list x 5              # x.. 5 → x + 4 ancestors (5 commits)
vc-x1 list x.. 5            # same as above
vc-x1 list ..x 3            # 2 descendants + x (3 commits)
vc-x1 list ..x.. 3          # 1 descendant, x, 1 ancestor (3 commits)
vc-x1 list ..x.. 1          # just x (1 commit)
```

**Defaults:** with no arguments, REVISION is `@` and COMMITS is 1
(just the working copy).

Named flags `-r`/`--revision`, `-n`/`--commits`, and `-R`/`--repo`
take precedence over positional arguments.

### Multi-repo queries

The `-R`/`--repo` flag can be repeated or comma-separated to query
multiple repos at once. When multiple repos are given, output is
labeled with bold `=== path ===` headers by default:

```
vc-x1 chid -R . -R .claude      # repeated flag
vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude         # comma-separated
vc-x1 list @.. 3 -R .,.claude   # works with all read-only subcommands
```

Control the label between repos with `-l`/`--label` and `-L`/`--no-label`:

```
vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude            # default label: === path ===
vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude -l "---"   # custom label:  --- path ---
vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude -L         # no label (raw output)
```

Examples:

```
$ vc-x1 chid -R . -R .claude
=== . ===
kwoyvposvsmv

=== .claude ===
zzpksklxyrnw

$ vc-x1 chid -R . -R .claude -L
kwoyvposvsmv
zzpksklxyrnw

$ vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude
=== . ===
kwoyvposvsmv

=== .claude ===
zzpksklxyrnw

$ vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude -L
kwoyvposvsmv
zzpksklxyrnw

$ vc-x1 list @.. 3 -R .,.claude
=== . ===
kwoyvposvsmv eae175c3c1b5 (no description set)
pqxtxxpnsmot b0c46930a640 Reorganize notes (0.19.1)
ssokyszzwsxw eeb15bc6d839 Unify .. notation and CLI (0.19.0)

=== .claude ===
zzpksklxyrnw 2388185e42ee (no description set)
tzupykyyvnrp 81ce22e34d41 Reorganize notes (0.19.1)
slmkmroqtqtp 1c33ccaa567d Unify .. notation and CLI (0.19.0)

$ vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude -L
kwoyvposvsmv
zzpksklxyrnw

$ vc-x1 desc -r @- -R .,.claude
=== . ===
pqxtxxpnsmot b0c46930a640 Reorganize notes: move older done items to done.md, update todos (0.19.1)

    Move completed items [1]-[13] and their references from todo.md to done.md.
    Replace completed jj-organization todo with new items.

    ochid: tzupykyyvnrp

=== .claude ===
tzupykyyvnrp 81ce22e34d41 Reorganize notes: move older done items to done.md, update todos (0.19.1)

    Session: reviewed and committed notes reorganization for 0.19.1.

    ochid: pqxtxxpnsmot

$ vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude -l "---"
--- . ---
kwoyvposvsmv

--- .claude ---
zzpksklxyrnw
```

Most decoration strings work unquoted (`---`, `===`, `>>>`, `:::`,
`+++`). Use single quotes for strings containing shell metacharacters
like `*`, `!`, `#`, `$`, or `~` (e.g. `-l '***'`). Double quotes
won't protect against `!` (bash history expansion).

With a single repo (or no `-R`), no label is printed — backward
compatible with previous behavior. Multi-repo is supported for
`chid`, `desc`, `list`, and `show`. `finalize` remains single-repo.

### finalize

Solves the session repo coherence problem: when the bot commits `.claude`,
the act of committing generates more session data. `finalize` waits,
then squashes the trailing writes into the target commit.

By default finalize runs in the foreground. Use `--detach` to run in
the background (the bot uses this so the session can end immediately).

The bot's last action in a session:

```
vc-x1 finalize --repo .claude --bookmark main --delay 10 --detach --push
```

If there is non-written session data after a session ends (e.g.
finalize failed or was skipped), run it manually:

```
vc-x1 finalize --repo .claude --bookmark main --push
```

This runs in the foreground, squashes, advances the bookmark, and pushes.

See [finalize subcommand](./notes/chores-01.md#finalize-subcommand-for-session-repo-coherence)
for full option reference and design details.

### Testing finalize

Always test against a throwaway jj repo, never the live workspace.
Use `mktemp -d` for unique directories so results can't be confused
with a previous run:

```bash
# Create and init a temp repo
dir=$(mktemp -d /tmp/vc-x1-test-XXXXXX)
(cd "$dir" && jj git init)

# Foreground (default, blocks, logs to test dir)
vc-x1 finalize --repo "$dir" --bookmark main --log "$dir/finalize.log"
cat "$dir/finalize.log"

# Detached (returns immediately, child logs to test dir)
dir2=$(mktemp -d /tmp/vc-x1-test-XXXXXX)
(cd "$dir2" && jj git init)
vc-x1 finalize --detach --repo "$dir2" --bookmark main --log "$dir2/finalize.log"
cat "$dir2/finalize.log"
```

The log file shows timestamped (nanoseconds) entries with PIDs, covering
the full flow: `main` entry/exit, `finalize` entry/exit, `detach`
spawn, and `finalize_exec` in the child process.

## Cross-repo Linking with Git Trailers

Commits in each repo use [git trailers](https://git-scm.com/docs/git-interpret-trailers)
to cross-reference their counterpart in the other repo. The `ochid`
(Other Change ID) trailer contains a workspace-root-relative path
and jj changeID:

```
ochid: /.claude/xvzvruqo   # points to a .claude repo change
ochid: /wtpmottv            # points to an app repo change
```

Paths always start with `/` (the workspace root, i.e. vc-x1).
Each repo has a `.vc-config.toml` that identifies its location
within the workspace, so tools can resolve these paths locally.

For full details see:
- [Git trailer convention](./notes/chores-01.md#git-trailer-convention)
  — [ochid (Other Change ID)](./notes/chores-01.md#ochid-other-change-id)
  — [ChangeID path syntax](./notes/chores-01.md#changeid-path-syntax)
  — [.vc-config.toml](./notes/chores-01.md#vc-configtoml)

## jj Tips for Git Users

If you're coming from git, jj's log output can be surprising compared to
tools like `gitk --all`.

### Why `jj log` shows fewer commits than `gitk`

jj tracks *changes* (identified by change IDs), not individual git commits.
When you rewrite a change (`jj describe`, `jj rebase`, `jj squash`, etc.),
jj creates a new git commit and keeps the old one under `refs/jj/keep/*` as
undo history. `gitk --all` sees all of these obsolete commits; `jj log` only
shows the current version of each change.

### Useful commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `jj log` | Show recent visible commits (default revset) |
| `jj log -r ::@` | Show **all** ancestors of the working copy |
| `jj log -r 'all()'` | Show all non-hidden commits (needed if you have multiple heads/branches) |
| `jj obslog -r <change-id>` | Show the evolution history of a single change |
| `jj op log` | Show operation history (each rewrite operation) |

In a single-branch workflow, `jj log -r ::@` and `jj log -r 'all()'` give
the same result. Use `all()` when you have multiple branches or heads.

## License

Licensed under either of

- Apache License, Version 2.0 ([LICENSE-APACHE](LICENSE-APACHE) or http://apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0)
- MIT license ([LICENSE-MIT](LICENSE-MIT) or http://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)

### Contribution

Unless you explicitly state otherwise, any contribution intentionally submitted
for inclusion in the work by you, as defined in the Apache-2.0 license, shall
be dual licensed as above, without any additional terms or conditions.

I see `fix-ochid` isn't documented in the Usage section at all — it was added in 0.22.0 but never added to the README. Here's what I'd propose adding after the `finalize` line in the usage block:

```
vc-x1 fix-ochid --other-repo <PATH> [OPTS]  # Validate/fix ochid trailers
```

And a `### fix-ochid` section after the `### Testing finalize` section covering the key flags: `--other-repo`, `--no-dry-run`, `--add-missing`, `--fallback`, `--id-len`, `--title`.

Want me to draft that for your review before editing?

- [License](#license)

This is experiment 1 to explore creating a Vibe Coding (vc) environment.
We will investigate ways of using the dual jj-git repo concept, explored
in [hw-jjg-bot](https://github.com/winksaville/hw-jjg-bot.git) to
initially make it easy to see how the code base evolved. This is
made possible by the fact that we have two repos one with the code
and one with the conversation with the bot.

I've chosen the jj-git environment because jj provides the concept that
each commit has an immutable changeID as well as the mutable commitID
of git. The idea is that each commit made on repo A writes the
changeID in the commit message to repo B. Thus there is a cross reference
between the two repos and this will allow vc-x1 to show how the repo
evolved and the entity (bot or human) can more clearly understand **how** and
most importantly **why** the code evolved.

The solution space is wide open, from trivial CLI, web or app based
(mobile/non-mobile). In addition, I could see this as an extension to
existing programming editors like vscode and zed or even creating our
own IDE for vc.

See [Initial commit with dual jj-git repos](./notes/chores-01.md#initial-commit-with-dual-jj-git-repos)
for how the initial commit was created with the dual jj-git repos. After
doing so and I then created this README.md file.

## Usage

```
vc-x1 list [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # List commits in a jj repo
vc-x1 desc [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # Show full description of a commit
vc-x1 chid [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # Print changeID(s) for a revision
vc-x1 show [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # Show commit details and diff summary
vc-x1 finalize --bookmark <B> [OPTS]   # Squash working copy into target
vc-x1 --version                        # Print version
vc-x1 --help                           # Print help
```

**The `..` notation:** dots on the revision show which direction to
list commits:

- `x..` — x at top, ancestors below (older commits)
- `..x` — descendants above (newer commits), x at bottom
- `..x..` — both directions, x in the middle

COMMITS is the total number of commits to show (x is always included).
Without dots, `x` with a count defaults to `x..` (ancestors).
For `..x..`, the budget is split: ancestors get the extra on odd counts.

```
vc-x1 list -r @              # just @ (1 commit)
vc-x1 list -r @.. -n 5       # @ + 4 ancestors (5 commits, @ at top)
vc-x1 list -r ..@ -n 3       # 2 descendants + @ (3 commits, @ at bottom)
vc-x1 list -r ..@.. -n 3     # 1 descendant, @, 1 ancestor (3 commits)
vc-x1 list -r ..@.. -n 4     # 1 descendant, @, 2 ancestors (4 commits)
```

### Positional shorthand

REVISION and COMMITS can be given as positional arguments, omitting
`-r` and `-n`:

```
vc-x1 list x                # just x (1 commit)
vc-x1 list x 5              # x.. 5 → x + 4 ancestors (5 commits)
vc-x1 list x.. 5            # same as above
vc-x1 list ..x 3            # 2 descendants + x (3 commits)
vc-x1 list ..x.. 3          # 1 descendant, x, 1 ancestor (3 commits)
vc-x1 list ..x.. 1          # just x (1 commit)
```

**Defaults:** with no arguments, REVISION is `@` and COMMITS is 1
(just the working copy).

Named flags `-r`/`--revision`, `-n`/`--commits`, and `-R`/`--repo`
take precedence over positional arguments.

### Multi-repo queries

The `-R`/`--repo` flag can be repeated or comma-separated to query
multiple repos at once. When multiple repos are given, output is
labeled with bold `=== path ===` headers by default:

```
vc-x1 chid -R . -R .claude      # repeated flag
vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude         # comma-separated
vc-x1 list @.. 3 -R .,.claude   # works with all read-only subcommands
```

Control the label between repos with `-l`/`--label` and `-L`/`--no-label`:

```
vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude            # default label: === path ===
vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude -l "---"   # custom label:  --- path ---
vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude -L         # no label (raw output)
```

Examples:

```
$ vc-x1 chid -R . -R .claude
=== . ===
kwoyvposvsmv

=== .claude ===
zzpksklxyrnw

$ vc-x1 chid -R . -R .claude -L
kwoyvposvsmv
zzpksklxyrnw

$ vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude
=== . ===
kwoyvposvsmv

=== .claude ===
zzpksklxyrnw

$ vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude -L
kwoyvposvsmv
zzpksklxyrnw

$ vc-x1 list @.. 3 -R .,.claude
=== . ===
kwoyvposvsmv eae175c3c1b5 (no description set)
pqxtxxpnsmot b0c46930a640 Reorganize notes (0.19.1)
ssokyszzwsxw eeb15bc6d839 Unify .. notation and CLI (0.19.0)

=== .claude ===
zzpksklxyrnw 2388185e42ee (no description set)
tzupykyyvnrp 81ce22e34d41 Reorganize notes (0.19.1)
slmkmroqtqtp 1c33ccaa567d Unify .. notation and CLI (0.19.0)

$ vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude -L
kwoyvposvsmv
zzpksklxyrnw

$ vc-x1 desc -r @- -R .,.claude
=== . ===
pqxtxxpnsmot b0c46930a640 Reorganize notes: move older done items to done.md, update todos (0.19.1)

    Move completed items [1]-[13] and their references from todo.md to done.md.
    Replace completed jj-organization todo with new items.

    ochid: tzupykyyvnrp

=== .claude ===
tzupykyyvnrp 81ce22e34d41 Reorganize notes: move older done items to done.md, update todos (0.19.1)

    Session: reviewed and committed notes reorganization for 0.19.1.

    ochid: pqxtxxpnsmot

$ vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude -l "---"
--- . ---
kwoyvposvsmv

--- .claude ---
zzpksklxyrnw
```

Most decoration strings work unquoted (`---`, `===`, `>>>`, `:::`,
`+++`). Use single quotes for strings containing shell metacharacters
like `*`, `!`, `#`, `$`, or `~` (e.g. `-l '***'`). Double quotes
won't protect against `!` (bash history expansion).

With a single repo (or no `-R`), no label is printed — backward
compatible with previous behavior. Multi-repo is supported for
`chid`, `desc`, `list`, and `show`. `finalize` remains single-repo.

### finalize

Solves the session repo coherence problem: when the bot commits `.claude`,
the act of committing generates more session data. `finalize` waits,
then squashes the trailing writes into the target commit.

By default finalize runs in the foreground. Use `--detach` to run in
the background (the bot uses this so the session can end immediately).

The bot's last action in a session:

```
vc-x1 finalize --repo .claude --bookmark main --delay 10 --detach --push
```

If there is non-written session data after a session ends (e.g.
finalize failed or was skipped), run it manually:

```
vc-x1 finalize --repo .claude --bookmark main --push
```

This runs in the foreground, squashes, advances the bookmark, and pushes.

See [finalize subcommand](./notes/chores-01.md#finalize-subcommand-for-session-repo-coherence)
for full option reference and design details.

### Testing finalize

Always test against a throwaway jj repo, never the live workspace.
Use `mktemp -d` for unique directories so results can't be confused
with a previous run:

```bash
# Create and init a temp repo
dir=$(mktemp -d /tmp/vc-x1-test-XXXXXX)
(cd "$dir" && jj git init)

# Foreground (default, blocks, logs to test dir)
vc-x1 finalize --repo "$dir" --bookmark main --log "$dir/finalize.log"
cat "$dir/finalize.log"

# Detached (returns immediately, child logs to test dir)
dir2=$(mktemp -d /tmp/vc-x1-test-XXXXXX)
(cd "$dir2" && jj git init)
vc-x1 finalize --detach --repo "$dir2" --bookmark main --log "$dir2/finalize.log"
cat "$dir2/finalize.log"
```

The log file shows timestamped (nanoseconds) entries with PIDs, covering
the full flow: `main` entry/exit, `finalize` entry/exit, `detach`
spawn, and `finalize_exec` in the child process.

## Cross-repo Linking with Git Trailers

Commits in each repo use [git trailers](https://git-scm.com/docs/git-interpret-trailers)
to cross-reference their counterpart in the other repo. The `ochid`
(Other Change ID) trailer contains a workspace-root-relative path
and jj changeID:

```
ochid: /.claude/xvzvruqo   # points to a .claude repo change
ochid: /wtpmottv            # points to an app repo change
```

Paths always start with `/` (the workspace root, i.e. vc-x1).
Each repo has a `.vc-config.toml` that identifies its location
within the workspace, so tools can resolve these paths locally.

For full details see:
- [Git trailer convention](./notes/chores-01.md#git-trailer-convention)
  — [ochid (Other Change ID)](./notes/chores-01.md#ochid-other-change-id)
  — [ChangeID path syntax](./notes/chores-01.md#changeid-path-syntax)
  — [.vc-config.toml](./notes/chores-01.md#vc-configtoml)

## jj Tips for Git Users

If you're coming from git, jj's log output can be surprising compared to
tools like `gitk --all`.

### Why `jj log` shows fewer commits than `gitk`

jj tracks *changes* (identified by change IDs), not individual git commits.
When you rewrite a change (`jj describe`, `jj rebase`, `jj squash`, etc.),
jj creates a new git commit and keeps the old one under `refs/jj/keep/*` as
undo history. `gitk --all` sees all of these obsolete commits; `jj log` only
shows the current version of each change.

### Useful commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `jj log` | Show recent visible commits (default revset) |
| `jj log -r ::@` | Show **all** ancestors of the working copy |
| `jj log -r 'all()'` | Show all non-hidden commits (needed if you have multiple heads/branches) |
| `jj obslog -r <change-id>` | Show the evolution history of a single change |
| `jj op log` | Show operation history (each rewrite operation) |

In a single-branch workflow, `jj log -r ::@` and `jj log -r 'all()'` give
the same result. Use `all()` when you have multiple branches or heads.

input.old_string: ### finalize
input.new_string:
### fix-ochid

Validates and fixes ochid trailers across commit history. Checks three
properties: correct path prefix, correct changeID length, and that the
ID resolves in the other repo. Default is dry-run — use `--no-dry-run`
to write changes.

```
# Dry-run: show what would be fixed
vc-x1 fix-ochid -r @.. --other-repo .claude

# Actually fix
vc-x1 fix-ochid -r @.. --other-repo .claude --no-dry-run

# Add missing ochid trailers by matching title + timestamp (within 60s)
vc-x1 fix-ochid -r @.. --other-repo .claude --add-missing

# Use a fallback for IDs not found in other repo
vc-x1 fix-ochid -r @.. --other-repo .claude --fallback /.claude/lost
```

Key flags:

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--other-repo <PATH>` | Path to the counterpart repo (required) |
| `--no-dry-run` | Write fixes (default is dry-run) |
| `--add-missing` | Infer and add ochid for commits without one |
| `--fallback <VALUE>` | Replacement for IDs not found in other repo |
| `--id-len <N>` | Expected changeID length (default 12) |
| `--title <TEXT>` | Replace commit title at the same time |

The `--add-missing` flag matches commits by exact title and committer
timestamp within 60 seconds. It only adds the trailer when exactly one
match is found — ambiguous or zero matches are skipped.

### finalize

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# vc-x1

- [Overview](#vc-x1)
- [Usage](#usage)
  - [Revision shortcuts](#revision-shortcuts)
  - [finalize](#finalize)
  - [Testing finalize](#testing-finalize)
- [Cross-repo Linking with Git Trailers](#cross-repo-linking-with-git-trailers)
- [jj Tips for Git Users](#jj-tips-for-git-users)
- [License](#license)

This is experiment 1 to explore creating a Vibe Coding (vc) environment.
We will investigate ways of using the dual jj-git repo concept, explored
in [hw-jjg-bot](https://github.com/winksaville/hw-jjg-bot.git) to
initially make it easy to see how the code base evolved. This is
made possible by the fact that we have two repos one with the code
and one with the conversation with the bot.

I've chosen the jj-git environment because jj provides the concept that
each commit has an immutable changeID as well as the mutable commitID
of git. The idea is that each commit made on repo A writes the
changeID in the commit message to repo B. Thus there is a cross reference
between the two repos and this will allow vc-x1 to show how the repo
evolved and the entity (bot or human) can more clearly understand **how** and
most importantly **why** the code evolved.

The solution space is wide open, from trivial CLI, web or app based
(mobile/non-mobile). In addition, I could see this as an extension to
existing programming editors like vscode and zed or even creating our
own IDE for vc.

See [Initial commit with dual jj-git repos](./notes/chores-01.md#initial-commit-with-dual-jj-git-repos)
for how the initial commit was created with the dual jj-git repos. After
doing so and I then created this README.md file.

## Usage

```
vc-x1 list [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # List commits in a jj repo
vc-x1 desc [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # Show full description of a commit
vc-x1 chid [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # Print changeID(s) for a revision
vc-x1 show [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # Show commit details and diff summary
vc-x1 fix-ochid --other-repo <PATH> [OPTS]  # Validate/fix ochid trailers
vc-x1 finalize --bookmark <B> [OPTS]       # Squash working copy into target
vc-x1 --version                            # Print version
vc-x1 --help                           # Print help
```

**The `..` notation:** dots on the revision show which direction to
list commits:

- `x..` — x at top, ancestors below (older commits)
- `..x` — descendants above (newer commits), x at bottom
- `..x..` — both directions, x in the middle

COMMITS is the total number of commits to show (x is always included).
Without dots, `x` with a count defaults to `x..` (ancestors).
For `..x..`, the budget is split: ancestors get the extra on odd counts.

```
vc-x1 list -r @              # just @ (1 commit)
vc-x1 list -r @.. -n 5       # @ + 4 ancestors (5 commits, @ at top)
vc-x1 list -r ..@ -n 3       # 2 descendants + @ (3 commits, @ at bottom)
vc-x1 list -r ..@.. -n 3     # 1 descendant, @, 1 ancestor (3 commits)
vc-x1 list -r ..@.. -n 4     # 1 descendant, @, 2 ancestors (4 commits)
```

### Positional shorthand

REVISION and COMMITS can be given as positional arguments, omitting
`-r` and `-n`:

```
vc-x1 list x                # just x (1 commit)
vc-x1 list x 5              # x.. 5 → x + 4 ancestors (5 commits)
vc-x1 list x.. 5            # same as above
vc-x1 list ..x 3            # 2 descendants + x (3 commits)
vc-x1 list ..x.. 3          # 1 descendant, x, 1 ancestor (3 commits)
vc-x1 list ..x.. 1          # just x (1 commit)
```

**Defaults:** with no arguments, REVISION is `@` and COMMITS is 1
(just the working copy).

Named flags `-r`/`--revision`, `-n`/`--commits`, and `-R`/`--repo`
take precedence over positional arguments.

### Multi-repo queries

The `-R`/`--repo` flag can be repeated or comma-separated to query
multiple repos at once. When multiple repos are given, output is
labeled with bold `=== path ===` headers by default:

```
vc-x1 chid -R . -R .claude      # repeated flag
vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude         # comma-separated
vc-x1 list @.. 3 -R .,.claude   # works with all read-only subcommands
```

Control the label between repos with `-l`/`--label` and `-L`/`--no-label`:

```
vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude            # default label: === path ===
vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude -l "---"   # custom label:  --- path ---
vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude -L         # no label (raw output)
```

Examples:

```
$ vc-x1 chid -R . -R .claude
=== . ===
kwoyvposvsmv

=== .claude ===
zzpksklxyrnw

$ vc-x1 chid -R . -R .claude -L
kwoyvposvsmv
zzpksklxyrnw

$ vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude
=== . ===
kwoyvposvsmv

=== .claude ===
zzpksklxyrnw

$ vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude -L
kwoyvposvsmv
zzpksklxyrnw

$ vc-x1 list @.. 3 -R .,.claude
=== . ===
kwoyvposvsmv eae175c3c1b5 (no description set)
pqxtxxpnsmot b0c46930a640 Reorganize notes (0.19.1)
ssokyszzwsxw eeb15bc6d839 Unify .. notation and CLI (0.19.0)

=== .claude ===
zzpksklxyrnw 2388185e42ee (no description set)
tzupykyyvnrp 81ce22e34d41 Reorganize notes (0.19.1)
slmkmroqtqtp 1c33ccaa567d Unify .. notation and CLI (0.19.0)

$ vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude -L
kwoyvposvsmv
zzpksklxyrnw

$ vc-x1 desc -r @- -R .,.claude
=== . ===
pqxtxxpnsmot b0c46930a640 Reorganize notes: move older done items to done.md, update todos (0.19.1)

    Move completed items [1]-[13] and their references from todo.md to done.md.
    Replace completed jj-organization todo with new items.

    ochid: tzupykyyvnrp

=== .claude ===
tzupykyyvnrp 81ce22e34d41 Reorganize notes: move older done items to done.md, update todos (0.19.1)

    Session: reviewed and committed notes reorganization for 0.19.1.

    ochid: pqxtxxpnsmot

$ vc-x1 chid -R .,.claude -l "---"
--- . ---
kwoyvposvsmv

--- .claude ---
zzpksklxyrnw
```

Most decoration strings work unquoted (`---`, `===`, `>>>`, `:::`,
`+++`). Use single quotes for strings containing shell metacharacters
like `*`, `!`, `#`, `$`, or `~` (e.g. `-l '***'`). Double quotes
won't protect against `!` (bash history expansion).

With a single repo (or no `-R`), no label is printed — backward
compatible with previous behavior. Multi-repo is supported for
`chid`, `desc`, `list`, and `show`. `finalize` remains single-repo.

### fix-ochid

Validates and fixes ochid trailers across commit history. Checks three
properties: correct path prefix, correct changeID length, and that the
ID resolves in the other repo. Default is dry-run — use `--no-dry-run`
to write changes.

```
# Dry-run: show what would be fixed
vc-x1 fix-ochid -r @.. --other-repo .claude

# Actually fix
vc-x1 fix-ochid -r @.. --other-repo .claude --no-dry-run

# Add missing ochid trailers by matching title + timestamp (within 60s)
vc-x1 fix-ochid -r @.. --other-repo .claude --add-missing

# Use a fallback for IDs not found in other repo
vc-x1 fix-ochid -r @.. --other-repo .claude --fallback /.claude/lost
```

Key flags:

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--other-repo <PATH>` | Path to the counterpart repo (required) |
| `--no-dry-run` | Write fixes (default is dry-run) |
| `--add-missing` | Infer and add ochid for commits without one |
| `--fallback <VALUE>` | Replacement for IDs not found in other repo |
| `--id-len <N>` | Expected changeID length (default 12) |
| `--title <TEXT>` | Replace commit title at the same time |

The `--add-missing` flag matches commits by exact title and committer
timestamp within 60 seconds. It only adds the trailer when exactly one
match is found — ambiguous or zero matches are skipped.

### finalize

Solves the session repo coherence problem: when the bot commits `.claude`,
the act of committing generates more session data. `finalize` waits,
then squashes the trailing writes into the target commit.

By default finalize runs in the foreground. Use `--detach` to run in
the background (the bot uses this so the session can end immediately).

The bot's last action in a session:

```
vc-x1 finalize --repo .claude --bookmark main --delay 10 --detach --push
```

If there is non-written session data after a session ends (e.g.
finalize failed or was skipped), run it manually:

```
vc-x1 finalize --repo .claude --bookmark main --push
```

This runs in the foreground, squashes, advances the bookmark, and pushes.

See [finalize subcommand](./notes/chores-01.md#finalize-subcommand-for-session-repo-coherence)
for full option reference and design details.

### Testing finalize

Always test against a throwaway jj repo, never the live workspace.
Use `mktemp -d` for unique directories so results can't be confused
with a previous run:

```bash
# Create and init a temp repo
dir=$(mktemp -d /tmp/vc-x1-test-XXXXXX)
(cd "$dir" && jj git init)

# Foreground (default, blocks, logs to test dir)
vc-x1 finalize --repo "$dir" --bookmark main --log "$dir/finalize.log"
cat "$dir/finalize.log"

# Detached (returns immediately, child logs to test dir)
dir2=$(mktemp -d /tmp/vc-x1-test-XXXXXX)
(cd "$dir2" && jj git init)
vc-x1 finalize --detach --repo "$dir2" --bookmark main --log "$dir2/finalize.log"
cat "$dir2/finalize.log"
```

The log file shows timestamped (nanoseconds) entries with PIDs, covering
the full flow: `main` entry/exit, `finalize` entry/exit, `detach`
spawn, and `finalize_exec` in the child process.

## Cross-repo Linking with Git Trailers

Commits in each repo use [git trailers](https://git-scm.com/docs/git-interpret-trailers)
to cross-reference their counterpart in the other repo. The `ochid`
(Other Change ID) trailer contains a workspace-root-relative path
and jj changeID:

```
ochid: /.claude/xvzvruqo   # points to a .claude repo change
ochid: /wtpmottv            # points to an app repo change
```

Paths always start with `/` (the workspace root, i.e. vc-x1).
Each repo has a `.vc-config.toml` that identifies its location
within the workspace, so tools can resolve these paths locally.

For full details see:
- [Git trailer convention](./notes/chores-01.md#git-trailer-convention)
  — [ochid (Other Change ID)](./notes/chores-01.md#ochid-other-change-id)
  — [ChangeID path syntax](./notes/chores-01.md#changeid-path-syntax)
  — [.vc-config.toml](./notes/chores-01.md#vc-configtoml)

## jj Tips for Git Users

If you're coming from git, jj's log output can be surprising compared to
tools like `gitk --all`.

### Why `jj log` shows fewer commits than `gitk`

jj tracks *changes* (identified by change IDs), not individual git commits.
When you rewrite a change (`jj describe`, `jj rebase`, `jj squash`, etc.),
jj creates a new git commit and keeps the old one under `refs/jj/keep/*` as
undo history. `gitk --all` sees all of these obsolete commits; `jj log` only
shows the current version of each change.

### Useful commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `jj log` | Show recent visible commits (default revset) |
| `jj log -r ::@` | Show **all** ancestors of the working copy |
| `jj log -r 'all()'` | Show all non-hidden commits (needed if you have multiple heads/branches) |
| `jj obslog -r <change-id>` | Show the evolution history of a single change |
| `jj op log` | Show operation history (each rewrite operation) |

In a single-branch workflow, `jj log -r ::@` and `jj log -r 'all()'` give
the same result. Use `all()` when you have multiple branches or heads.

## License

Licensed under either of

- Apache License, Version 2.0 ([LICENSE-APACHE](LICENSE-APACHE) or http://apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0)
- MIT license ([LICENSE-MIT](LICENSE-MIT) or http://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)

### Contribution

Unless you explicitly state otherwise, any contribution intentionally submitted
for inclusion in the work by you, as defined in the Apache-2.0 license, shall
be dual licensed as above, without any additional terms or conditions.

content.text:
I see the issues:

1. **Ordering**: 0.23.0 appears before 0.22.2 — they should be in version order
2. **Heading levels**: The versioned entries (0.20.1–0.23.0) are all `###` under topic groupings like `## Test dispersal and ochid list column`. Your suggestion is that `0.xx.0` (minor version bumps = new features) should be `##` level, with patch versions as `###` under them.

So the structure would be:
```
## 0.22.0 — Add fix-ochid subcommand
### 0.22.1 — Fix fix-ochid prefix bug
### 0.22.2 — Fix fix-ochid short ID extension
## 0.23.0 — Add --add-missing to fix-ochid
```

And the older topic-based groupings (`## Test dispersal and ochid list column`, `## Show subcommand`) would become `## 0.20.0`, `## 0.18.0`, etc.

Should I restructure the whole versioned section this way, or just fix the 0.22–0.23 area?

content.content:
404→## Show subcommand (0.18.0)
   405→
   406→### 0.18.0 — Initial show subcommand
   407→
   408→Implement `vc-x1 show` matching `jj show` output: commit/change IDs,
   409→bookmarks (local + remote), author/committer with timestamps,
   410→indented description, and file-level diff summary (Added/Modified/Removed).
   411→Uses `TreeDiffIterator` for sync tree diffing via jj-lib.
   412→
   413→### 0.18.1 — Flesh out show header
   414→
   415→Show header now has gitk-style fields: Ids, Author, Committer, Parent,
   416→Child, Branches (ancestry-based), Follows/Precedes (nearest tags),
   417→Description (body only). Supports `..` notation for multi-commit display
   418→with separators. File list: `-f` flag (default 50, `0`=none, `all`=unlimited)
   419→with first/last split when truncated. Remove `--indent` from desc,
   420→hardcode 4-space indent in both desc and show.
   421→
   422→### 0.19.0 — Unify `..` notation and CLI across all subcommands
   423→
   424→- Extract `collect_ids` in `common.rs` — returns `(Vec<CommitId>, usize)`,
   425→  ordered commit IDs and anchor index. Handles all `..` directions.
   426→- Extract `resolve_spec` — converts positional/flag args into `DotSpec`
   427→  (desc_count/anc_count) ready for `collect_ids`. Single code path for
   428→  all subcommands.
   429→- Replace `DotDirection` enum with `desc_count`/`anc_count` on `DotSpec`.
   430→- Remove `resolve_dot_args`, `ResolvedArgs`, `collect_both`/`collect_commit_lines`,
   431→  `show_both` — all replaced by `resolve_spec` + `collect_ids`.
   432→- All subcommands (chid, desc, list, show) now share one loop pattern.
   433→- `-r` flag supports `..` notation (e.g. `-r ..mpl -n 3`).
   434→- Rename `-l`/`--limit` to `-n`/`--commits` (`-l`/`--limit` kept as
   435→  hidden aliases). Positional `COMMITS` replaces `POS_COUNT`.
   436→- Count means total commits including anchor (e.g. `x.. 3` = x + 2
   437→  ancestors). Fixes off-by-one in descendants and bare-rev-with-count.
   438→- File list truncation: show first N files only. Skip truncation when
   439→  only 1 file would be omitted.
   440→- Raw jj revset syntax (`::`, `|`, `&`) no longer supported via `-r`;
   441→  use `..` notation instead.
   442→
   443→## Test dispersal and ochid list column
   444→
   445→### 0.20.1 — Disperse CLI parsing tests
   446→
   447→Move all CLI parsing tests from main.rs into per-subcommand test modules
   448→(chid.rs, desc.rs, list.rs, show.rs, finalize.rs). main.rs retains only
   449→the `unknown_command` test. Make `Commands` enum `pub(crate)` so submodule
   450→tests can match on it. Each module has a local `parse()` helper that
   451→returns the typed args struct directly, eliminating the `if let` boilerplate.
   452→
   453→### 0.21.0 — Show ochid in list output
   454→
   455→Replace commitID with ochid trailer value in `list` output. New column
   456→format: `chid  ochid  title` with 2-space gaps between columns. The ochid
   457→column is padded to `-w`/`--width` (default 21, fits `/.claude/` + 12-char
   458→changeID). Commits without an ochid trailer show a blank placeholder to
   459→keep columns aligned. Add `extract_ochid()` and `format_commit_with_ochid()`
   460→to common.rs. Also clean up CLI help: remove duplicate manual defaults from
   461→doc comments (clap shows `[default: ...]` automatically), use `default_value`
   462→for `--label`, and simplify `Header` enum from three variants to two
   463→(`Label(String)` and `None`).
   464→
   465→### 0.21.1 — Deduplicate common CLI flags
   466→
   467→Extract shared CLI args (pos_rev, pos_count, revision, repos, limit, label,
   468→no_label) into `CommonArgs` struct in common.rs using clap `#[command(flatten)]`.
   469→All four read-only subcommands (chid, desc, list, show) flatten it; list adds
   470→`width`, show adds `files`. Eliminates ~30 lines of duplicated arg definitions
   471→per subcommand. Tests updated to access fields via `.common`.
   472→
   473→### 0.22.0 — Add fix-ochid subcommand
   474→
   475→New `fix-ochid` subcommand to validate and fix ochid trailers in commit
   476→descriptions. Validates three properties: correct path prefix (derived from
   477→`--other-repo`), correct changeID length (`--id-len`, default 12), and that
   478→the ID resolves in the other repo. Supports `..` notation for revision ranges.
   479→Default is dry-run; `--no-dry-run` to actually write. `--fallback` provides a
   480→replacement value for IDs not found in the other repo (e.g. `/.claude/lost`
   481→for commits whose `.claude` history was lost to squashing). Optional `--title`
   482→to fix commit titles at the same time. Uses `jj describe --ignore-immutable`
   483→to rewrite descriptions.
   484→
   485→### 0.22.1 — Fix fix-ochid prefix bug
   486→
   487→fix-ochid computed the ochid prefix from the raw `--other-repo` CLI path
   488→(e.g. `../` produced `/../`). Now reads `workspace.path` from the other
   489→repo's `.vc-config.toml`, which is the authoritative source for the
   490→workspace-root-relative path. Added `toml_simple` module with `toml_load()`
   491→(returns `HashMap<String, String>`) and `toml_get()` for minimal TOML
   492→parsing — designed to be swapped for a real TOML crate later.
   493→
   494→### 0.23.0 — Add --add-missing to fix-ochid
   495→
   496→New `--add-missing` flag for `fix-ochid` that adds ochid trailers to commits
   497→that don't have one. Matches commits by title (exact) and committer timestamp
   498→(within 60 seconds). Only adds the trailer when exactly one match is found in
   499→the other repo — ambiguous or zero matches are skipped. Respects `--no-dry-run`.
   500→
   501→### 0.22.2 — Fix fix-ochid short ID extension
   502→
   503→fix-ochid could only truncate IDs to `--id-len`, not extend them. When the

## Show subcommand (0.18.0)

### 0.18.0 — Initial show subcommand

Implement `vc-x1 show` matching `jj show` output: commit/change IDs,
bookmarks (local + remote), author/committer with timestamps,
indented description, and file-level diff summary (Added/Modified/Removed).
Uses `TreeDiffIterator` for sync tree diffing via jj-lib.

### 0.18.1 — Flesh out show header

Show header now has gitk-style fields: Ids, Author, Committer, Parent,
Child, Branches (ancestry-based), Follows/Precedes (nearest tags),
Description (body only). Supports `..` notation for multi-commit display
with separators. File list: `-f` flag (default 50, `0`=none, `all`=unlimited)
with first/last split when truncated. Remove `--indent` from desc,
hardcode 4-space indent in both desc and show.

### 0.19.0 — Unify `..` notation and CLI across all subcommands

- Extract `collect_ids` in `common.rs` — returns `(Vec<CommitId>, usize)`,
  ordered commit IDs and anchor index. Handles all `..` directions.
- Extract `resolve_spec` — converts positional/flag args into `DotSpec`
  (desc_count/anc_count) ready for `collect_ids`. Single code path for
  all subcommands.
- Replace `DotDirection` enum with `desc_count`/`anc_count` on `DotSpec`.
- Remove `resolve_dot_args`, `ResolvedArgs`, `collect_both`/`collect_commit_lines`,
  `show_both` — all replaced by `resolve_spec` + `collect_ids`.
- All subcommands (chid, desc, list, show) now share one loop pattern.
- `-r` flag supports `..` notation (e.g. `-r ..mpl -n 3`).
- Rename `-l`/`--limit` to `-n`/`--commits` (`-l`/`--limit` kept as
  hidden aliases). Positional `COMMITS` replaces `POS_COUNT`.
- Count means total commits including anchor (e.g. `x.. 3` = x + 2
  ancestors). Fixes off-by-one in descendants and bare-rev-with-count.
- File list truncation: show first N files only. Skip truncation when
  only 1 file would be omitted.
- Raw jj revset syntax (`::`, `|`, `&`) no longer supported via `-r`;
  use `..` notation instead.

## Test dispersal and ochid list column

### 0.20.1 — Disperse CLI parsing tests

Move all CLI parsing tests from main.rs into per-subcommand test modules
(chid.rs, desc.rs, list.rs, show.rs, finalize.rs). main.rs retains only
the `unknown_command` test. Make `Commands` enum `pub(crate)` so submodule
tests can match on it. Each module has a local `parse()` helper that
returns the typed args struct directly, eliminating the `if let` boilerplate.

### 0.21.0 — Show ochid in list output

Replace commitID with ochid trailer value in `list` output. New column
format: `chid  ochid  title` with 2-space gaps between columns. The ochid
column is padded to `-w`/`--width` (default 21, fits `/.claude/` + 12-char
changeID). Commits without an ochid trailer show a blank placeholder to
keep columns aligned. Add `extract_ochid()` and `format_commit_with_ochid()`
to common.rs. Also clean up CLI help: remove duplicate manual defaults from
doc comments (clap shows `[default: ...]` automatically), use `default_value`
for `--label`, and simplify `Header` enum from three variants to two
(`Label(String)` and `None`).

### 0.21.1 — Deduplicate common CLI flags

Extract shared CLI args (pos_rev, pos_count, revision, repos, limit, label,
no_label) into `CommonArgs` struct in common.rs using clap `#[command(flatten)]`.
All four read-only subcommands (chid, desc, list, show) flatten it; list adds
`width`, show adds `files`. Eliminates ~30 lines of duplicated arg definitions
per subcommand. Tests updated to access fields via `.common`.

### 0.22.0 — Add fix-ochid subcommand

New `fix-ochid` subcommand to validate and fix ochid trailers in commit
descriptions. Validates three properties: correct path prefix (derived from
`--other-repo`), correct changeID length (`--id-len`, default 12), and that
the ID resolves in the other repo. Supports `..` notation for revision ranges.
Default is dry-run; `--no-dry-run` to actually write. `--fallback` provides a
replacement value for IDs not found in the other repo (e.g. `/.claude/lost`
for commits whose `.claude` history was lost to squashing). Optional `--title`
to fix commit titles at the same time. Uses `jj describe --ignore-immutable`
to rewrite descriptions.

### 0.22.1 — Fix fix-ochid prefix bug

fix-ochid computed the ochid prefix from the raw `--other-repo` CLI path
(e.g. `../` produced `/../`). Now reads `workspace.path` from the other
repo's `.vc-config.toml`, which is the authoritative source for the
workspace-root-relative path. Added `toml_simple` module with `toml_load()`
(returns `HashMap<String, String>`) and `toml_get()` for minimal TOML
parsing — designed to be swapped for a real TOML crate later.

### 0.23.0 — Add --add-missing to fix-ochid

New `--add-missing` flag for `fix-ochid` that adds ochid trailers to commits
that don't have one. Matches commits by title (exact) and committer timestamp
(within 60 seconds). Only adds the trailer when exactly one match is found in
the other repo — ambiguous or zero matches are skipped. Respects `--no-dry-run`.

### 0.22.2 — Fix fix-ochid short ID extension

fix-ochid could only truncate IDs to `--id-len`, not extend them. When the
existing ochid had an 8-char ID and `id_len` was 12, the fix produced the
same 8-char ID — `jj describe` saw no change and said "Nothing changed."
Now resolves the short ID in the other repo to get the full change ID, then
truncates to `id_len`. Added `resolved_id` parameter to
`fix_ochid_in_description`. Also added notes update steps (6–8) to the
pre-commit checklist in CLAUDE.md.

## Git commit headers and jj change-id preservation

### How jj stores change IDs in git

jj stores the change ID as a non-standard git commit header called
`change-id`. This is part of the raw commit object, visible with
`git cat-file commit <hash>`:

```
$ git cat-file commit f22d6c45b9d9
tree 55d1626ba2ad932a568fd669d18cc19eb38510fa
parent 432b7494734a5bbe549cd796335d9e67756f07bc
author Wink Saville <wink@saville.com> 1773950107 -0700
committer Wink Saville <wink@saville.com> 1773950107 -0700
change-id mkoooktqrrqpllrkwxvosunuywplqsyq

Deduplicate common CLI flags with #[command(flatten)] (0.21.1)

Extract CommonArgs struct in common.rs shared by chid, desc, list, show.
List adds width, show adds files on top. Update tests to access via .common.

ochid: /.claude/lwwsmvroqzly
```

The `change-id` header sits alongside standard git headers (tree, parent,
author, committer). The value is the full reverse-hex encoding of the
change ID — jj's short form (`mkoooktqrrqp`) is a 12-char prefix of
the full value (`mkoooktqrrqpllrkwxvosunuywplqsyq`).

### Preservation guarantees

From the [jj Git compatibility docs](https://jj-vcs.github.io/jj/latest/git-compatibility/):

> Change IDs are stored in git commit headers as reverse hex encodings.
> This is a non-standard header and is not preserved by all git tooling.
> For example, the header is preserved by a `git commit --amend`, but is
> not preserved through a rebase operation. GitHub and other major forges
> seem to preserve them for the most part.

**What preserves change IDs:**
- `git push` / `git fetch` / `git clone` — the header is part of the
  commit object, transferred as-is
- `git commit --amend` — preserves non-standard headers
- GitHub / major forges — preserve them in storage and API

**What can strip change IDs:**
- `git rebase` — rewrites commits, drops non-standard headers
- `git cherry-pick` — creates new commits without the header
- GitHub merge-squash / rebase-merge — creates new commits server-side

### Configuration

Controlled by `git.write-change-id-header` (default `true` since jj 0.29.0).
Verify with:

```
$ jj config get git.write-change-id-header
true
```

### Implications for ochid cross-references

Since our ochid trailers reference change IDs in the *other* repo, the
cross-references are only as stable as the change-id headers in both repos.
A regular `git push` to GitHub and `git clone` back will preserve them,
but any git tooling that rewrites commits could break the chain.

### 0.21.0 — Show ochid in list output

Replace commitID with ochid trailer value in `list` output. New column
format: `chid  ochid  title` with 2-space gaps between columns. The ochid
column is padded to `-w`/`--width` (default 21, fits `/.claude/` + 12-char
changeID). Commits without an ochid trailer show a blank placeholder to
keep columns aligned. Add `extract_ochid()` and `format_commit_with_ochid()`
to common.rs. Also clean up CLI help: remove duplicate manual defaults from
doc comments (clap shows `[default: ...]` automatically), use `default_value`
for `--label`, and simplify `Header` enum from three variants to two
(`Label(String)` and `None`).

### 0.21.1 — Deduplicate common CLI flags

Extract shared CLI args (pos_rev, pos_count, revision, repos, limit, label,
no_label) into `CommonArgs` struct in common.rs using clap `#[command(flatten)]`.
All four read-only subcommands (chid, desc, list, show) flatten it; list adds
`width`, show adds `files`. Eliminates ~30 lines of duplicated arg definitions
per subcommand. Tests updated to access fields via `.common`.

### 0.22.0 — Add fix-ochid subcommand

New `fix-ochid` subcommand to validate and fix ochid trailers in commit
descriptions. Validates three properties: correct path prefix (derived from
`--other-repo`), correct changeID length (`--id-len`, default 12), and that
the ID resolves in the other repo. Supports `..` notation for revision ranges.
Default is dry-run; `--no-dry-run` to actually write. `--fallback` provides a
replacement value for IDs not found in the other repo (e.g. `/.claude/lost`
for commits whose `.claude` history was lost to squashing). Optional `--title`
to fix commit titles at the same time. Uses `jj describe --ignore-immutable`
to rewrite descriptions.

### 0.22.1 — Fix fix-ochid prefix bug

fix-ochid computed the ochid prefix from the raw `--other-repo` CLI path
(e.g. `../` produced `/../`). Now reads `workspace.path` from the other
repo's `.vc-config.toml`, which is the authoritative source for the
workspace-root-relative path. Added `toml_simple` module with `toml_load()`
(returns `HashMap<String, String>`) and `toml_get()` for minimal TOML
parsing — designed to be swapped for a real TOML crate later.

### 0.23.0 — Add --add-missing to fix-ochid

New `--add-missing` flag for `fix-ochid` that adds ochid trailers to commits
that don't have one. Matches commits by title (exact) and committer timestamp
(within 60 seconds). Only adds the trailer when exactly one match is found in
the other repo — ambiguous or zero matches are skipped. Respects `--no-dry-run`.

### 0.22.2 — Fix fix-ochid short ID extension

fix-ochid could only truncate IDs to `--id-len`, not extend them. When the
existing ochid had an 8-char ID and `id_len` was 12, the fix produced the
same 8-char ID — `jj describe` saw no change and said "Nothing changed."
Now resolves the short ID in the other repo to get the full change ID, then
truncates to `id_len`. Added `resolved_id` parameter to
`fix_ochid_in_description`. Also added notes update steps (6–8) to the
pre-commit checklist in CLAUDE.md.

bookmarks (local + remote), author/committer with timestamps,

with separators. File list: `-f` flag (default 50, `0`=none, `all`=unlimited)
with first/last split when truncated. Remove `--indent` from desc,
hardcode 4-space indent in both desc and show.
